# UC-12.4.12 · Terraform Plan Drift Detection

## Description

Scheduled CI `terraform plan` runs that show changes when no pipeline apply occurred—focused operational view vs UC-12.4.2.

## Value

Scheduled CI `terraform plan` runs that show changes when no pipeline apply occurred—focused operational view vs UC-12.4.2.

## Implementation

Nightly plan-only workflow for prod workspaces. Alert on any change. Auto-create drift remediation ticket.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Terraform CLI in CI, Terraform Cloud.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Plan JSON `resource_changes`, `plan_mode=scheduled`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Nightly plan-only workflow for prod workspaces. Alert on any change. Auto-create drift remediation ticket.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=iac sourcetype="terraform:plan_ci"
| where plan_mode="scheduled" AND (changes_add>0 OR changes_change>0 OR changes_destroy>0)
| table _time, workspace, changes_add, changes_change, changes_destroy, run_url
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Terraform Plan Drift Detection** — Scheduled CI `terraform plan` runs that show changes when no pipeline apply occurred—focused operational view vs UC-12.4.2.

Documented **Data sources**: Plan JSON `resource_changes`, `plan_mode=scheduled`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Terraform CLI in CI, Terraform Cloud. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: iac; **sourcetype**: terraform:plan_ci. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=iac, sourcetype="terraform:plan_ci". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where plan_mode="scheduled" AND (changes_add>0 OR changes_change>0 OR changes_destroy>0)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Terraform Plan Drift Detection**): table _time, workspace, changes_add, changes_change, changes_destroy, run_url
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with Terraform Cloud audit and run history. Verify field extractions and index permissions.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (drift plans), Timeline, Bar chart (by workspace).

## SPL

```spl
index=iac sourcetype="terraform:plan_ci"
| where plan_mode="scheduled" AND (changes_add>0 OR changes_change>0 OR changes_destroy>0)
| table _time, workspace, changes_add, changes_change, changes_destroy, run_url
| sort -_time
```

## Visualization

Table (drift plans), Timeline, Bar chart (by workspace).
